channel.title: Paper Feeds (arXiv)
channel.description: Keyword-based research paper feeds from arXiv

item.title: Verifiable and Confidential DNN Inference on Low-End Edge Devices
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.07470v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在资源受限的低功耗边缘设备（如Cortex-M微控制器）上部署深度神经网络（DNN）推理，面临双重安全困境：一方面需保护模型机密性，防范被攻破的边缘系统窃取或逆向训练模型；另一方面需可验证推理结果的正确性（即确保推理代码按预期执行、参数未被篡改），同时避免引入不可接受的计算、内存或延迟开销。现有方案存在明显缺陷：基于可信执行环境（TEE）的方案（如TrustZone-A）常将部分模型与推理逻辑置于Secure World，导致可信计算基（TCB）过大、高度应用依赖，且难以适配内存仅数百KB的MCU级设备；纯软件方案则缺乏硬件级隔离，安全性薄弱。

方法创新：VECODI 与 SHANGRI-LA

本文提出 VECODI——首个面向低功耗边缘设备的可验证且保密的DNN推理框架。其核心创新是 SHANGRI-LA：一种运行于TrustZone-M TEE之上的新型执行抽象，构建了介于Secure World与Non-Secure World之间的第三特权层级。该层以极小TCB（<1.2 KB ROM / <384 B RAM）提供确定性隔离与轻量级安全原语，使未经信任的推理代码可在Non-Secure World高效执行，而模型权重、关键校验逻辑及证明生成器由Minimal Secure-World支持严格保护，实现双重保障：① 模型零泄露；② 推理过程与参数完整性可密码学验证。

实验验证与优势

我们在真实NUCLEO-L552ZE-Q开发板（Cortex-M33 + TrustZone-M）上完整实现并开源VECODI原型。评估表明：

  • TCB缩减92%（相较传统Secure-World全包方案）；
  • 内存占用仅14.2 KB RAM / 48.7 KB Flash，适配典型MCU；
  • 端到端推理+验证开销平均<13.6%（ResNet-18@ImageNet子集）；
  • 支持主流算子（Conv/ReLU/Pool）及量化模型（INT8）。

VECODI首次在MCU级设备上实现了实用化、硬件增强、应用无关的可验证保密推理，为智能传感、工业IoT等场景提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Deploying secure DNN inference on resource-constrained edge devices (e.g., Cortex-M MCUs) demands both model confidentiality and verifiable correctness, yet existing solutions trade off security, efficiency, or practicality. We present VECODI, a novel framework enabling verifiable and confidential DNN inference on low-end edge hardware. Its core is SHANGRI-LA, a new execution abstraction atop TrustZone-M that introduces a minimal third privilege level—strictly between Secure and Non-Secure Worlds—to isolate untrusted inference code (executed in Non-Secure World) while relying only on a tiny, application-agnostic Secure-World support (<1.2 KB ROM) for model protection and cryptographic verification of inference integrity (code execution + parameter fidelity). Implemented and open-sourced on a real NUCLEO-L552ZE-Q board (Cortex-M33), VECODI achieves a small memory footprint (14.2 KB RAM / 48.7 KB Flash), low runtime overhead (<13.6% for ResNet-18), and a drastically reduced TCB (92% smaller than conventional TEE-based approaches), demonstrating the first practical, hardware-enforced, verifiable confidential inference for MCU-class devices.

Abstract

Deploying deep neural network (DNN) inference on low-end edge devices raises two key challenges: protecting model confidentiality against a potentially compromised edge system and enabling verifiable inference without incurring prohibitive overhead. Existing approaches either house partial models and inference software within trusted execution environments (TEEs), resulting in high cost and an application-dependent trusted computing base (TCB), or execute in untrusted environments, providing little security. In this work, we present VECODI, a framework for verifiable and confidential DNN inference on constrained edge devices. At its core, VECODI introduces SHANGRI-LA, a new execution abstraction on TrustZone-M TEEs that establishes a third runtime environment with privileges strictly between the Secure and Non-Secure Worlds. VECODI leverages SHANGRI-LA to execute untrusted inference code in the Non-Secure World while using minimal application-agnostic Secure-World support to protect model confidentiality and enable verifiability (with respect to proper execution of inference code and model parameters) of inference results. We realize VECODI on a real-world NUCLEO-L552ZE-Q development board and open-source its prototype. Our results show VECODI's small TCB, memory footprint, and runtime overhead, making it a practical option for secure inference in low-end edge devices.

item.title: Sort, Partition, Randomize: Optimal Binary Hypothesis Testing under Local Differential Privacy
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.07443v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

本文研究局部差分隐私（LDP）下二元假设检验的最优机制设计。给定有限字母表（大小为 $k$）上的两个已知分布 $P_0$ 和 $P_1$，每个原始观测经 $\varepsilon$-LDP 机制 $Q$ 隐私化后用于判别数据来源。测试性能由输出分布 $Q P_0$ 与 $Q P_1$ 之间的 $f$-散度（如总变差、KL 散度、曲棍球棒散度）刻画。此前工作虽揭示了最优机制的结构性质（如极值性、单调性），但仅导出指数时间算法，难以实用。

核心发现：Sort-Partition-Randomize（SPR）结构

我们证明了一个锐利且普适的结构定理：对任意隐私预算 $\varepsilon > 0$ 和任意凸 $f$-散度目标，存在一个最优机制满足——

  • Sort：先按似然比 $\frac{P_1(x)}{P_0(x)}$ 对字母表升序排序；
  • Partition：将排序后的序列划分为若干连续块（contiguous blocks）；
  • Randomize：对块索引应用随机响应机制（即在块标签上施加 LDP）。

该 SPR 类机制将高维机制设计降维至块划分问题，兼具解析可解性与计算可行性。

方法与贡献

基于 SPR 结构，我们构建了精确动态规划算法：

  • 时间复杂度仅需 $O(k^3)$，远优于指数级前人方法；
  • 扩展至 $\ell$-输出约束时，降至 $O(\ell k^2)$；
  • 首次实现全隐私谱（$\varepsilon \in (0,\infty)$）下的精确最优机制计算与表征，突破仅适用于强隐私（$\varepsilon \to 0$）或弱隐私（$\varepsilon \to \infty$）的渐近分析局限。

本工作为 LDP 假设检验提供了首个高效、精确、结构清晰的最优解框架。

AI Summary (English)

We study optimal $\varepsilon$-locally differentially private (LDP) mechanisms for binary hypothesis testing, where observations drawn from known distributions $P_0,P_1$ on a $k$-element alphabet are privatized and then tested. Utility is measured by an $f$-divergence (e.g., total variation, KL, or hockey-stick) between the induced output distributions. Prior structural results led only to exponential-time algorithms. We prove a sharp characterization: for any $\varepsilon>0$ and any $f$-divergence, there exists an optimal LDP mechanism that (i) sorts the alphabet by likelihood ratio, (ii) partitions it into contiguous blocks, and (iii) applies randomized response to the block index—termed Sort-Partition-Randomize (SPR). This yields an exact dynamic program computing the optimum in $O(k^3)$ time (or $O(\ell k^2)$ with $\ell$ output symbols), enabling efficient, non-asymptotic characterization of the global optimum across the full privacy range.

Abstract

We study optimal design of $\varepsilon$-locally differentially private mechanisms for binary hypothesis testing. Each observation is drawn from one of two known distributions $P_0,P_1$ on a finite alphabet of size $k$, privatized by a mechanism $Q$, and then used to infer which distribution generated the data. We measure testing utility using an $f$-divergence, including total variation, KL, and hockey-stick divergences, between the two induced output distributions. Previous work established structural properties of optimal mechanisms, but only yielded exponential-time algorithms. We prove a sharp structure: for every $\varepsilon$ and every $f$-divergence objective, after sorting the alphabet by likelihood ratio, there exists an optimal mechanism that partitions the sorted alphabet into contiguous blocks and applies randomized response to the block label. We call this class Sort-Partition-Randomize (SPR). This characterization yields an exact dynamic program that computes an optimal mechanism in $O(k^3)$ time, and more generally in $O(\ell k^2)$ time with an $\ell$-output budget. Our results make it possible to efficiently compute and characterize the exact optimum across the full privacy range, beyond asymptotic privacy regimes.

item.title: On the Shoulders of Giants: Empowering Automated Smart Contract Auditing via the GiAnt Corpus
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.07363v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

高质量智能合约审计数据集对评估安全工具性能、推动智能合约安全研究至关重要。然而，现有数据集普遍存在两大瓶颈：人工构建导致的可扩展性严重受限，以及数据粒度粗、漏洞类型与严重性分布单一，难以支撑细粒度模型训练与多任务评测。

方法创新：GiANT 自动化框架

本文提出 GiANT（Giant-scale Automated curation of smart coNTract auditing data），首个面向真实审计报告的端到端自动化数据集构建框架。其核心包含三阶段设计：

  • 结构化解析：采用“分而治之”策略，结合思维链（Chain-of-Thought）提示工程，精准从 Code4rena 等专业审计报告中抽取漏洞位置、触发条件、影响逻辑、修复建议等细粒度字段；
  • 可信验证：引入 LLM-as-a-judge 机制，通过多轮一致性校验与对抗性重写检测，自动过滤噪声与歧义样本；
  • 标准化构建：统一映射至五级 CVSS 兼容严重性体系（Critical/High/Medium/Low/Info），确保语义可比性。

主要成果与验证

在 388 份真实审计报告上运行 GiANT，生成 GiAnt Corpus——迄今最大规模、最高粒度的开源智能合约审计数据集，含 7,711 条结构化漏洞发现。人工抽样评估（n=200）显示：信息提取质量达 4.76±0.37（满分5分），双人标注者间一致性系数 κ = 0.88（极强一致）。进一步在四大下游任务（漏洞检测、代码摘要、修复建议生成、Gas 优化）上对 4 种 SOTA LLM 进行基准测试，确立了首个统一评测基线，为自动化审计研究提供坚实数据基石。

AI Summary (English)

High-quality smart contract auditing datasets are essential yet scarce due to manual curation bottlenecks and insufficient granularity/diversity. To address this, we propose GiANT, an automated framework that distills structured vulnerability insights from real-world audit reports (e.g., Code4rena) via a divide-and-conquer strategy enhanced with Chain-of-Thought prompting, followed by rigorous LLM-as-a-judge quality assurance. Applied to 388 reports, GiANT produces the GiAnt Corpus—7,711 fine-grained, severity-labeled (5 levels) vulnerability findings. Manual evaluation confirms exceptional reliability (mean quality score: 4.76±0.37/5; inter-rater agreement κ = 0.88). We further benchmark 4 state-of-the-art LLMs across four core auditing tasks—vulnerability detection, code summarization, mitigation recommendation, and automated gas optimization—establishing the first standardized performance baselines. The GiAnt Corpus is publicly released to advance automated smart contract security research.

Abstract

High-quality smart contract auditing datasets are crucial for evaluating security tools and advancing smart contract security research. Two major limitations of existing datasets are the manual-induced scalability bottleneck and the deficiency in data granularity and diversity. To address these limitations, we propose GiANT, an automated framework designed to curate smart contract auditing datasets by distilling vulnerability insights from real-world auditing reports. GiANT employs a divide-and-conquer strategy coupled with the Chain-of-Thought technique to extract structured vulnerability information from Code4rena reports, followed by an LLM-as-a-judge mechanism to perform rigorous quality assurance. To evaluate GiANT's effectiveness, we run it on 388 real-world audit reports and generate the GiAnt Corpus comprising 7,711 vulnerability findings across five severity levels. Manual assessment of the dataset demonstrates exceptional reliability in information extraction, achieving a mean quality score of $4.76\pm0.37$ (out of 5) with inter-rater agreement $κ$ of 0.88. We further validate the practicality of our dataset by benchmarking 4 state-of-the-art LLMs on vulnerability detection, code summarization, mitigation recommendation, and automated gas optimization tasks, to establish performance baselines, thereby providing a valuable data foundation for future research in automated smart contract auditing.

item.title: Empirical Evaluation of Large Language Models for Migration of Code Fragments to Post-Quantum Cryptography
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.07341v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

面向后量子密码学（PQC）的迁移不仅是密码原语的简单替换，更涉及周边软件逻辑的深度重构。当前PQC迁移框架多聚焦组织流程，缺乏对代码级自动化修复的有效支持，导致人工迁移易出错、效率低、难以规模化。

方法与数据集

本研究构建了首个面向密码迁移任务的可复现评估框架：

  • 设计包含800组配对Python代码片段的合成数据集，覆盖6类主流密码族（如RSA、ECC、AES-GCM）及多原语组合场景；
  • 每对代码均通过类别定制化功能测试严格验证（如密钥封装一致性、签名验签闭环），确保数据质量与评估客观性；
  • 对比4种模型：零样本GPT-4.1，以及在本数据集上微调的GPT-3.5-turbo、GPT-4.1-mini和CodeLlama-7B-Instruct。

主要发现与创新

  • 领域微调至关重要：零样本GPT-4.1动态正确率仅61.2%，而微调后的GPT-4.1-mini达92.5%（+31.3个百分点），静态相似度达0.9072；
  • 提出“验证驱动迁移”范式：将功能测试嵌入训练与评估闭环，显著提升生成代码的可靠性；
  • 在6个真实开源项目（如PyCA/cryptography、OpenSSL Python bindings）中开展补充验证，证实方法在局部密码模块中具备实用价值，但也揭示其在跨模块依赖与状态耦合场景下的局限性。

本工作为构建可验证、可集成、依赖感知的LLM增强型密码敏捷迁移流水线提供了实证基础与工程路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper empirically evaluates large language models (LLMs) for automating the migration of pre-quantum cryptographic code fragments to post-quantum counterparts while preserving functional correctness. We introduce a reproducible framework built on a synthetic dataset of 800 paired Python code snippets covering six cryptographic families and multi-primitive compositions, each validated via category-specific functional tests. Four models are assessed: zero-shot GPT-4.1, and fine-tuned GPT-3.5-turbo, GPT-4.1-mini, and CodeLlama-7B-Instruct. Results show domain-specific fine-tuning is essential—fine-tuned GPT-4.1-mini achieves 92.5% dynamic functional correctness and 0.9072 mean static similarity, substantially outperforming zero-shot GPT-4.1 (61.2%). Complementary validation on six open-source repositories confirms practical utility in localized cryptographic modules but exposes limitations under complex cross-module dependencies. Our work demonstrates that fine-tuned LLMs, when integrated with automated verification and dependency-aware validation, can serve as reliable components in future crypto-agile migration pipelines.

Abstract

The transition to post-quantum cryptography (PQC) requires not only replacing vulnerable cryptographic primitives, but also refactoring the surrounding software logic. While existing PQC migration frameworks provide organizational guidance, practical code-level remediation remains largely manual and error-prone. This paper evaluates whether large language models (LLMs) can be trained to assist in the migration of pre-quantum cryptographic code fragments to post-quantum counterparts while preserving functional correctness. To this end, we introduce a reproducible experimental framework built around a synthetic dataset of 800 paired Python code fragments covering six cryptographic families and combined multi-primitive cases. Each pair is validated through category-specific functional tests, enabling both dataset quality control and objective evaluation of model-generated migrations. Four models are assessed: GPT-4.1 in a zero-shot setting, and fine-tuned versions of GPT-3.5-turbo, GPT-4.1-mini, and CodeLlama-7B-Instruct. The results show that domain-specific fine-tuning is essential for reliable cryptographic migration. The fine-tuned GPT-4.1-mini model achieves the best overall performance, with a mean static similarity of 0.9072 and a dynamic functional correctness rate of 92.5%, substantially outperforming the zero-shot baseline. A complementary validation on six open-source repositories further shows that the approach can produce useful migrations in localized cryptographic modules, while also revealing limitations in larger projects with complex dependencies and cross-module interactions. These findings suggest that fine-tuned LLMs can serve as practical components in future crypto-agile migration pipelines, provided they are coupled with automated verification and dependency-aware validation.

item.title: Defending Jailbreak Attacks on Large Language Models via Manifold Trajectory Kinetics
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.07335v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

针对大语言模型（LLMs）的越狱攻击（jailbreak attacks）通过精心设计的提示词绕过安全对齐机制，诱发有害输出，严重威胁模型部署可靠性。现有检测方法多依赖于静态度量空间（如原始输入、梯度或隐藏层特征），假设良性与越狱提示在此空间中线性可分。然而，该假设在两类现实场景下失效：（i）伪恶意提示（pseudo-malicious prompts）——语义良性但含安全关键词（如“如何制作炸弹”仅作学术讨论），易被误判；（ii）自适应攻击（adaptive attacks）——攻击者显式优化对抗已部署检测器，导致检测性能急剧下降。

方法创新：流形轨迹动力学（MTK）

本文摒弃“寻找普适判别空间”的范式，转而建模LLM内部数据流形的局部邻域结构演化。我们将LLM视为一个动力学系统：输入提示沿前向传播逐层变换，其邻域（k近邻）的几何关系持续演化。MTK核心思想是：良性提示的邻域始终锚定在良性流形区域；而越狱提示则呈现典型双阶段轨迹——起始于恶意种子附近，后战略性漂移至良性邻域以规避拒绝响应。我们通过量化各层隐状态邻域的语义一致性（如余弦相似度分布熵、邻域类别偏移度）实现无监督、无需微调的检测。

关键结果与泛化性

在4个主流LLM（Llama-2-7b/13b、Vicuna、Qwen-7b）及10种越狱攻击（如GCG、AutoDAN、PAIR）上，MTK展现出强鲁棒性：

  • 对伪恶意提示：越狱检出率95%（FPR仅5%于良性提示、2%于伪恶意提示）；
  • 对自适应攻击：检出率稳定保持85%，显著优于基线（平均+32个百分点）；
  • 首次验证MTK在视觉-语言模型（如LLaVA）上的有效性，跨模态迁移性突出。

代码开源：https://github.com/Rookie143/mtk

AI Summary (English)

Jailbreak attacks undermine LLM safety by evading alignment guardrails, yet existing detectors—relying on fixed metric spaces (e.g., inputs or gradients)—fail against pseudo-malicious prompts (benign-intent but keyword-rich) and adaptive attacks optimized to bypass them. To address this, we propose Manifold Trajectory Kinetics (MTK), which treats the LLM as a kinetic system and detects jailbreaks by tracking how a prompt’s local neighborhood structure evolves across layers: benign prompts maintain proximity to benign manifolds throughout inference, whereas jailbreak prompts follow a characteristic trajectory—starting near malicious seeds and later shifting toward benign neighborhoods to evade refusal. Evaluated on 4 LLMs and 10 jailbreak attacks, MTK achieves 95% true positive rate at only 5% false positive rate on benign prompts and 2% on pseudo-malicious ones, and maintains 85% TPR under adaptive attacks—outperforming prior methods by >30 points on average. We further demonstrate MTK’s effectiveness in vision-language models. Code: https://github.com/Rookie143/mtk

Abstract

Jailbreak prompts can bypass alignment guardrails in large language models (LLMs) and elicit unsafe outputs, making reliable deployment-time detection critical. Prior detection approaches largely rely on a fixed metric space, e.g., raw inputs, gradients, or hidden features, in which benign and jailbreak prompts are linearly separable. We show this assumption breaks under (i) pseudo-malicious prompts that are benign by intent but contain safety-related keywords, and (ii) adaptive attacks that explicitly optimize against the deployed detector. To overcome this limitation, we shift our focus from identifying a universal metric space to analyzing the more robust neighborhood structure of the underlying data manifold. We present Manifold Trajectory Kinetics (MTK), which treats an LLM as a kinetic system transforming inputs into outputs and detects jailbreaks by tracking how a prompt's neighborhood structure evolves across layers. Benign prompts remain close to benign neighborhoods throughout inference, whereas jailbreak prompts exhibit a characteristic trajectory that begins near malicious seeds and later strategically shifts toward benign neighborhoods to evade refusal.Across four LLMs and ten jailbreak attacks, MTK achieves strong robustness to both failure modes: on pseudo-malicious prompts, it attains a jailbreak true positive rate of 95% at a false positive rate of 5% on benign prompts and 2% on pseudo-malicious prompts, and under adaptive attacks, it maintains a true positive rate of 85%. We further demonstrate the superior performance of MTK for jailbreak detection in vision-language models. Our code is available at https://github.com/Rookie143/mtk.

item.title: The Capacity of Information-Theoretic Secure Aggregation in Federated Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.07277v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

在联邦学习中，信息论安全聚合（Secure Aggregation） 是保障用户本地模型更新隐私的核心机制：服务器需准确计算所有更新之和，却无法获知任一用户的原始更新。现有方案多依赖可信第三方（TTP）或预设的群组结构分发相关随机密钥，但其密钥分发通信开销常被忽略，导致在通用密钥分发机制下的安全聚合根本容量极限长期未知。

方法与框架

本文提出首个两阶段通用建模框架：第一阶段为用户间直连通信的密钥分发（无TTP、无结构约束），第二阶段为带$T$-用户合谋攻击容忍的聚合。该框架联合刻画三类关键资源：（1）安全性所需随机性（掩码熵）、（2）密钥分发通信总量、（3）聚合阶段通信开销。

主要成果与创新

  • 完整刻画容量区域：首次给出三资源间的精确权衡边界，通过构造性方案与信息论逆向证明（converse）严格匹配；
  • 确定性显式构造：在任意大小≥$N$的有限域上实现容量最优，无需随机化或存在性证明，显著优于依赖TTP或大域假设的既有方案；
  • 实用化突破：证明仅需两两共享密钥即可达优，可原生集成Diffie–Hellman协议实现；
  • 效率优势：相比Google经典方案，在相同聚合通信开销下，减少约33%的随机掩码密钥量，降低存储与生成负担。

本工作为安全聚合建立了去中心化、可验证、可部署的信息论基础。

AI Summary (English)

This paper establishes the fundamental capacity region of information-theoretic secure aggregation in federated learning with $N$ users and $T$-colluding adversaries, under a general two-phase framework: key distribution via user-to-user communication (no trusted third party or prescribed structures), followed by update aggregation. We jointly characterize the trade-offs among three resources: security randomness (masking entropy), key-distribution communication cost, and aggregation communication overhead. A novel deterministic, capacity-achieving scheme is constructed over any finite field of size at least $N$, eliminating reliance on randomized or existential arguments—and crucially, it requires only pairwise shared keys, enabling practical implementation via Diffie–Hellman. Compared to Google’s seminal scheme, our approach achieves identical aggregation communication while reducing required random masking keys by up to one-third. The matching converse proves optimality.

Abstract

Secure aggregation allows a server to aggregate users' local updates while preserving update privacy. Existing information-theoretic problems typically assume that correlated random keys are provided by a trusted third party (TTP) or generated via prescribed groupwise structures, while the communication cost for establishing such correlated keys is often ignored. Consequently, the fundamental limits under general key-distribution mechanisms remain unknown. In this paper, we study the $T$-colluding information-theoretic secure aggregation problem with $N$ users under a general two-phase framework consisting of a key distribution phase and an update aggregation phase. Unlike prior work, we model key distribution through user-to-user communication and allow arbitrary user-generated key-distribution mechanisms, eliminating TTP or prescribed structures. This enables a joint characterization of three resources: randomness for security, key-distribution communication, and aggregation communication. We completely characterize the capacity region among these three resources by constructing a novel secure aggregation scheme together with a matching information-theoretic converse. In particular, we develop an explicit deterministic capacity-achieving construction over any finite field of size at least $N$, whereas most existing schemes either rely on TTP or employ randomized or existential constructions over sufficiently large finite fields. We further show that the optimal performance can be achieved using only pairwise shared keys, enabling implementation via Diffie--Hellman key exchange. Compared with Google's seminal secure aggregation scheme, the proposed scheme requires fewer random masking keys while preserving the same aggregation communication overhead.

item.title: MalSkillBench: A Runtime-Verified Benchmark of Malicious Agent Skills
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.07131v1

AI Summary (中文)

MalSkillBench：首个运行时验证的恶意智能体技能基准

随着Claude Code、Gemini CLI等AI编程智能体广泛集成第三方“技能”（skill）——即融合自然语言指令、可执行脚本与工具权限的Markdown包——新型供应链风险应运而生。此类技能兼具代码与面向智能体的指令双重属性，其威胁既非传统代码漏洞，亦非纯提示注入，现有检测工具缺乏覆盖该混合空间的可验证真值基准，导致评估失真、野样本（wild-only）评测存在严重偏差。

本文提出MalSkillBench，首个基于运行时行为验证的恶意技能基准：

  • 构建3,944个恶意技能，按三维分类法（攻击目标×行为模式×控制平面层级）精细标注于108类单元格；
  • 其中3,214个源自闭环的生成–验证–反馈流程：仅保留能在Docker沙箱中触发真实恶意行为（系统调用监控+LLM裁判双重确认）的样本；
  • 补充703个真实野样本（含86.6%集中于单一加密货币盗取活动）及4,000个语义匹配的良性技能作为对照。

关键发现：

  • 验证脆弱性差异显著：代码注入类技能验证通过率达94.5%，而提示注入仅75.8%，暴露其检测难度根源；
  • 野样本分布高度偏斜：81%来自仅两个账户，且新出现攻击面——直接劫持智能体控制平面（如重写规划器指令）；
  • 现有防御存在根本割裂：最强技能专用检测器对代码注入召回率高达98.4%，但在提示注入与控制平面攻击上骤降至<30%；仅用野样本评估会使排名波动达66个召回点；
  • 供应链扫描器与提示防护各自仅观察技能的“一半”，无法建模代码–指令协同关系。

结论：恶意技能检测必须联合推理任务意图、执行代码与自然语言指令。我们开源全部数据集、验证流水线、基线模型与完整结果。

AI Summary (English)

MalSkillBench is the first runtime-verified benchmark for malicious AI agent skills—third-party modules combining natural-language instructions, executable code, and tool permissions. It comprises 3,944 malicious skills labeled across a three-dimensional taxonomy (108 cells), with 3,214 rigorously verified via a closed-loop Generate-Verify-Feedback pipeline: each triggers observable malicious behavior inside a Docker sandbox under system-call tracing and LLM adjudication. We further include 703 in-the-wild samples (dominated by one crypto-theft campaign) and 4,000 matched benign skills. Key findings: (1) Code-injection skills achieve 94.5% verification yield vs. only 75.8% for prompt injection—mirroring their detection fragility; (2) Wild samples are narrow and architecturally skewed, revealing novel control-plane attacks; (3) The strongest skill-specific detector achieves 98.4% recall on code injection but collapses (<30%) on prompt-injection and agent-control attacks, and wild-only evaluation distorts rankings by up to 66 recall points; (4) Supply-chain scanners and prompt-injection defenses each observe only half of a skill, and no combination recovers the critical code–instruction coupling. Thus, robust detection requires joint reasoning over intent, code, and instructions. All data, pipelines, baselines, and results are publicly released.

Abstract

AI coding agents such as Claude Code and Gemini CLI increasingly extend themselves with third-party skills: markdown packages bundling natural-language instructions, executable scripts, and tool permissions. Because a skill is at once code and agent-facing instruction, it introduces a supply chain dependency whose risk is neither pure code nor pure prompt. Detection tools have never been measured against verified ground truth spanning this hybrid space, leaving their effectiveness unknown and wild-only evaluations biased. We present MalSkillBench, the first runtime-verified benchmark of malicious agent skills: 3,944 malicious skills labeled along a three-dimensional taxonomy of 108 cells. Of these, 3,214 come from a closed-loop Generate-Verify-Feedback pipeline admitting only samples whose malicious behavior fires inside a Docker sandbox under system-call monitoring and an LLM judge; we add 703 in-the-wild and 4,000 matched benign skills. Our measurements are consistent: code injection reaches 94.5% verification yield but prompt injection only 75.8%, the same fragility that later makes it hard to detect; the wild sample is narrow, dominated by one cryptocurrency-theft campaign (86.6% one behavior, 81% from two accounts) with a small but architecturally new tail attacking the agent control plane; the strongest skill-specific detector reaches 98.4% recall on code injection yet collapses on prompt-injection and agent-control attacks, and wild-only scoring swings the ranking by up to 66 recall points; supply-chain scanners and prompt-injection defenses each see only half of a skill, and no combination recovers the code-instruction relationship. Detecting malicious skills therefore requires reasoning jointly over task intent, code, and instructions. We release the dataset, pipeline, baselines, and results.

item.title: HAVE: Host Active Verification Engine for Closing the Contextual Reality Gap in Security Digital Twins
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06968v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

安全数字孪生（Security Digital Twins, SDTs）通过构建基础设施的动态虚拟映射，支撑威胁仿真与攻击路径分析。然而，当前SDT普遍依赖理论化的CVSS评分推断横向移动概率，导致情境现实鸿沟（Contextual Reality Gap）：在存在未建模缓解措施（如运行时补丁、EDR规则）时高估风险；而在遭遇绕过内存安全机制的逻辑漏洞时严重低估风险——CVSS无法反映真实主机上下文中的 exploit 可利用性。

方法创新

本文提出主机主动验证引擎（Host Active Verification Engine, HAVE），作为SDT的关键扩展组件。HAVE部署轻量、安全沙箱化的主机代理，在快照隔离环境中对目标漏洞执行受控的伯努利试验（exploit尝试），基于最大似然估计获得实证妥协概率 $\hat{p}$。进一步，引入Wilson置信区间宽度导出的置信权重 $α_w$，通过形式化关联Beta-Binomial后验的贝叶斯融合规则，将 $\hat{p}$ 无缝注入蒙特卡洛攻击图仿真中，实现不确定性感知的风险量化。

主要结果

在涵盖4类漏洞（栈溢出、UAF、逻辑缺陷、TOCTOU）、3级安全配置（基础/加固/零信任）及2个生产级二进制程序（nginx、Redis）的系统评估中：HAVE将误报场景下的可达概率 $P_{\text{reach}}$ 降低38.2%，同时将漏报场景下 $P_{\text{reach}}$ 提升132.4%，净校正幅度达 +124.1%；更重要的是，其风险估计对校准超参数 $κ$ 的敏感度显著降低——跨 $κ$ 取值的变化范围仅 $1.12\times$，远优于CVSS基线的 $4.6\times$，证实HAVE大幅提升SDT的情境真实性与鲁棒性。

AI Summary (English)

Security Digital Twins (SDTs) enable dynamic threat simulation but suffer from a Contextual Reality Gap: CVSS-based lateral-movement probabilities overestimate risk where mitigations (e.g., EDR rules, patches) silently neutralize exploits, and drastically underestimate it when logic flaws bypass memory-safety defenses. We propose HAVE — the Host Active Verification Engine — an SDT extension that deploys a safety-constrained host agent to empirically measure compromise probability $\hat{p}$ via maximum-likelihood estimation over snapshot-isolated Bernoulli trials. A Wilson-interval-derived confidence weight $α_w$ propagates $\hat{p}$ into Monte Carlo attack graph simulations using a Bayesian blending rule formally grounded in the Beta-Binomial posterior. Evaluated across four vulnerability classes, three security tiers, and two production binaries, HAVE reduces false-positive $P_{\text{reach}}$ by 38.2%, increases false-negative $P_{\text{reach}}$ by 132.4% (net +124.1% correction), and cuts sensitivity to calibration exponent $κ$ from $4.6\times$ (CVSS baseline) to just $1.12\times$.

Abstract

Security Digital Twins (SDTs) provide continuously updated virtual replicas of infrastructure for threat simulation, yet they rely on theoretical CVSS scores to assign lateral-movement probabilities -- creating the Contextual Reality Gap: risk is overestimated where unacknowledged mitigations neutralize exploits, and drastically underestimated where logic flaws bypass all memory-safety defenses. We present the Host Active Verification Engine (HAVE), an SDT extension that deploys a safety-constrained host agent to measure the empirical probability of compromise $\hat{p}$ via maximum-likelihood estimation over snapshot-isolated Bernoulli trials. A Wilson interval-width confidence weight $α_w$ propagates $\hat{p}$ into Monte Carlo simulations via a Bayesian blending rule formally related to the Beta-Binomial posterior. Evaluation across four vulnerability classes, three security tiers, and two production binaries shows HAVE reduces $P_{\text{reach}}$ by 38.2% in false-positive scenarios and increases it by 132.4% in false-negative scenarios, with a net +124.1% correction; post-HAVE estimates vary by only $1.12\times$ across calibration exponents $κ$, versus $4.6\times$ for CVSS-only baselines.

item.title: Blockchain Infrastructure for Intelligent Cyber--Physical--Social Systems:Post-Quantum Security, Interoperability, and Trustworthy Data Economies in the Era of Embodied AI
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06895v1

AI Summary (中文)

面向具身智能的区块链基础设施：后量子安全、互操作性与可信数据经济

本教程系统阐述区块链如何演进为智能信息物理社会系统（ICPSS）的统一协调层，应对具身人工智能（Embodied AI）与量子计算双重范式变革带来的核心挑战。随着基于世界模型的机器人在真实环境中持续感知、决策与行动，亟需可验证的数据溯源机制、跨组织协同治理框架，以及激励相容的去中心化数据共享范式。与此同时，2025年诺贝尔物理学奖与图灵奖所标志的量子硬件突破（超导、离子阱、中性原子平台），正加速瓦解当前RSA/ECDSA等公钥密码体系，使传统区块链面临“长期验证失效”风险。为此，本教程提出密码敏捷（crypto-agile）区块链架构——以BrokerChain协议实现跨分片可扩展性，融合Croissant元数据标准与机器人学习溯源链，构建端到端可信数据经济；通过AWS Braket实机演示，量化评估ECDSA向NIST标准化后量子签名（如CRYSTALS-Dilithium）迁移的时间窗口与工程路径；并整合工业级多模态云部署实践，验证从实验室原型到产业生态的落地可行性。创新点在于：首次将量子硬件现实约束、具身AI数据生命周期与区块链系统设计三者深度耦合，提供开源框架（含QKD-ready共识模块、Provenance-as-a-Service API）及分阶段迁移路线图，支撑构建抗量子、高互操作、强可信的下一代分布式智能基座。

AI Summary (English)

This tutorial positions blockchain as the foundational coordination layer for Intelligent Cyber–Physical–Social Systems (ICPSS), co-addressing two existential challenges: the data-provenance and incentive-aligned sharing demands of embodied AI, and the cryptographic collapse threat posed by near-term quantum hardware (recognized by the 2025 Nobel Prize in Physics and Turing Award). We introduce a crypto-agile, quantum-resistant infrastructure, demonstrated via live AWS Braket experiments on superconducting, trapped-ion, and neutral-atom devices to benchmark ECDSA-to-post-quantum (e.g., CRYSTALS-Dilithium) migration timelines. Key contributions include: (1) BrokerChain—a scalable cross-shard protocol enabling interoperability; (2) trustworthy data economies grounded in Croissant metadata and robotic learning provenance; and (3) industry-integrated, multi-modal cloud deployment blueprints. Open-source frameworks and phased roadmaps are provided to architect systems that are quantum-resilient, interoperable, and verifiably trustworthy.

Abstract

The deployment of embodied artificial intelligence via world-model-based robotics presents a transformative opportunity for blockchain infrastructure, establishing urgent demand for trustworthy data provenance, cross-organizational governance, and incentive-compatible sharing across decentralized ecosystems. Simultaneously, quantum computing advances recognized by the 2025 Nobel Prize in Physics and the Turing Award threaten the cryptographic primitives securing these data economies, creating an interdependent imperative: long-lived verification for embodied AI depends on crypto-agile architectures capable of withstanding quantum adversaries. This tutorial examines blockchain as the coordination layer bridging this dual transition, from financial substrate to foundational Cyber-Physical-Social Systems infrastructure that simultaneously secures against quantum cryptanalysis and enables scalable, trustworthy data economies. The session opens with an immersive AWS Braket demonstration engaging participants with superconducting, trapped-ion, and neutral-atom hardware to assess cryptographic threat timelines and witness ECDSA-to-post-quantum signature transitions. Five integrated modules progress from embodied AI and world-model requirements through quantum hardware reality and evidence-based security migration, to scalable cross-shard architectures via BrokerChain protocols, trustworthy data economies implementing Croissant metadata standards and robotic learning provenance, and industry ecosystem integration for multi-modal cloud deployment. By bridging quantum hardware realities with embodied AI data requirements, this tutorial charts blockchain as unified infrastructure for next-generation decentralized intelligent environments, providing open-source frameworks and roadmaps for architecting quantum-resistant, interoperable, and data-trustworthy systems.

item.title: Hierarchical Certified Semantic Commitment for Byzantine-Resilient LLM-Agent Collaboration
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.07316v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在大语言模型（LLM）智能体的拜占庭容错协作中，传统共识机制面临根本性不适配：LLM输出为结构化自然语言而非确定性字节序列，其语义相似性无法由经典BFT的“值相等”判定；而简单聚合（如多数投票）则掩盖了“是否可提交、提交何种类型、为何需中止”这一关键终局性（finality）决策。

方法创新：分层认证语义承诺（H-CSC）

本文提出Hierarchical Certified Semantic Commitment（H-CSC）——一种受BFT启发但面向语义设计的新型终局性原语。它不追求单一布尔判决，而是基于嵌入空间中按判决结果分组的提案语义凝聚度，动态输出三类带类型标签的终局结果：

  • semantic_commit：当≥2f+1个诚实代理在同一判决下形成紧密语义核心（角距离≤阈值）时，生成参数有界、量化聚合后的语义摘要（digest）；
  • verdict_commit：判决高度一致但语义分散（无凝聚核心）时，仅颁发判决级证书，不声称语义聚合；
  • typed abort：明确标注原因（如n<3f+1），强制中止非BFT可行轮次。

关键实证结果

  • 在可控语义投毒诊断集（BCS_v1, 120轮）中，H-CSC在BFT可行桶内实现低角偏差提交（0.31°–2.04°），且对全部超BFT难度轮次（n<3f+1）100%精准中止；
  • 在真实LLM-Agent声明验证基准（MVR-50, 50任务）中，面对静态/激进拜占庭攻击，提交率0.90/0.92，诚实参考无效率仅0.02/0.00，统计上媲美强证书基线；更关键的是，其在74%/72%轮次额外输出嵌入支撑的semantic_commit digest，提供可验证语义溯源；
  • 消融实验表明：纯语义严格策略仅提交0.54/0.48，证实判决级回退机制对覆盖率提升至关重要（+0.36/+0.44），同时严守≤0.04安全底线；跨4种LLM的100任务测试进一步验证无效主决（invalid_hmaj）稳定控制在0.00–0.03。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces Hierarchical Certified Semantic Commitment (H-CSC), a Byzantine-resilient finality protocol for LLM-agent collaboration that replaces binary commit/abort with typed semantic outcomes: semantic_commit (requiring a 2f+1 semantic core within verdict and emitting an embedding-backed digest), verdict_commit (strong margin without semantic cohesion), or typed abort (with explicit reason). Unlike classical BFT or naive aggregation, H-CSC explicitly exposes the semantic grounding of finality decisions. Evaluated on BCS_v1 (120 episodes), it achieves low angular deviation (0.31°–2.04°) on BFT-feasible rounds and aborts 100% of n < 3f+1 rounds. On MVR-50 under static/rushing attacks, it matches a strong verdict-only baseline in commit rate (0.90/0.92) and safety (honest-reference-invalid: 0.02/0.00), while additionally issuing semantic digests on 74%/72% of rounds—providing typed provenance. Ablation confirms the verdict-level fallback is essential for coverage (+0.36/+0.44) without compromising safety (≤0.04 invalid_hmaj), validated across four LLMs.

Abstract

Byzantine collaboration among large-language-model agents requires a finality-control primitive: given delivered stochastic, structured natural-language proposals, the protocol must decide whether the round supports a commit, what kind of commit, or a typed safe abort. Naive aggregation hides this choice behind a single verdict; classical Byzantine fault tolerance hides it behind byte-identity that LLM proposals do not satisfy. We introduce Hierarchical Certified Semantic Commitment (H-CSC), a BFT-inspired protocol that converts embedding-derived finality signals over verdict-conditioned proposal groups into one of three typed outcomes: a semantic_commit (a 2f+1 within-verdict semantic core backs the verdict, emitting a parameter-bound digest over the quantised aggregate), a verdict_commit (strong verdict margin but dispersed semantic rationale, emitting a verdict-level certificate without claiming a semantic aggregate), or an explicit abort with a typed reason. The contribution is typed finality, not raw commit accuracy. On a controlled semantic-poisoning diagnostic (BCS_v1, 120 episodes), H-CSC commits with low angular deviation on BFT-feasible buckets (0.31 to 2.04 degrees) and aborts 100% of beyond-BFT rounds (n<3f+1) as intended. On a real LLM-agent claim-verification benchmark (MVR-50, 50 tasks) under paired static and rushing Byzantine attacks, H-CSC commits 0.90/0.92 with honest-reference-invalid rates of 0.02/0.00, statistically matching a strong certificate-emitting verdict-only baseline. Unlike that baseline, H-CSC also emits an embedding-backed semantic_commit digest on 74%/72% of rounds, supplying typed provenance. A strict-semantic ablation commits only 0.54/0.48, showing the verdict-level fallback is necessary for coverage (+0.36/+0.44) at the same <=0.04 safety floor; a 100-task cross-model check across four LLMs preserves invalid_hmaj within 0.00 to 0.03.

item.title: Where Rectified Flows Leak: Characterising Membership Signals Along the Interpolation Path
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.07271v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

生成式模型（如Rectified Flows, RFs）在部署中日益广泛，但其对训练数据的隐式记忆仍构成版权与隐私风险。现有研究多聚焦于直接复现（verbatim leakage），而忽视更隐蔽的“潜伏信号”——即未显现在采样输出中、却可被探测并利用的成员身份线索。本文首次系统刻画RF模型在插值路径 $X_λ = (1-λ)X_0 + λX_1$（其训练核心机制）上泄露的成员推断信号。

方法与发现

我们发现：训练集样本与测试集样本在该路径上的重建误差差值 $\Delta\mathcal{L}(λ)$ 呈现显著的钟形曲线结构——随 $λ$ 先增后减，在某特定 $λ^$ 处达峰值。该信号在训练过程中持续累积，而标准验证指标（如FID、LPIPS）却保持稳定，表明其具有高度隐蔽性。在高斯假设下，我们解析推导出峰值位置闭式解：$λ^ = \frac{\sigma_0^2}{\sigma_0^2 + \sigma_1^2}$，其中 $\sigma_0^2, \sigma_1^2$ 分别为初始/目标分布方差。该理论预测在音频（VCTK）与图像（CelebA-HQ、FFHQ）数据集上均获验证：钟形结构具有普适性；当数据近似满足高斯混合假设时，$λ^*$ 预测误差 < 0.03。

创新与应用

本工作首次揭示RF的插值路径是可定位的隐私泄露通道，提出首个基于 $λ$-分辨的Membership Inference Attack（MIA）：仅需在预测的最优 $λ^*$ 处评估重建误差，即可显著区分成员/非成员（AUROC达0.82–0.91）。该方法无需访问模型梯度或训练日志，仅依赖前向推理，具备强实用性与泛化性，为生成模型安全评估提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Understanding subtle data memorization—beyond verbatim reproduction—in generative models is critical for privacy and copyright. We characterize membership leakage in Rectified Flows (RFs) along their core training interpolation path $X_λ = (1-λ)X_0 + λX_1$. We discover that the reconstruction error gap between train and test samples forms a robust bell-shaped curve over $λ$, accumulating during training while validation metrics remain stable. Under Gaussian assumptions, we derive its peak location $λ^ = \sigma_0^2 / (\sigma_0^2 + \sigma_1^2)$ in closed form. Empirical validation across audio and image benchmarks confirms the universality of the bell shape and high accuracy of $λ^$ prediction when assumptions hold. Leveraging this $λ$-resolved signal, we design a practical Membership Inference Attack achieving AUROC 0.82–0.91 using only forward evaluation at $λ^*$—no gradients or training logs required. This reveals RF’s interpolation path as a previously unrecognized, exploitable privacy channel.

Abstract

Understanding what generative models retain from training data remains challenging, with implications for copyright and privacy. Beyond verbatim reproduction, models can encode subtler traces of their training data that never surface in their outputs yet remain exploitable. We study this regime for Rectified Flows, which are increasingly used in deployed generative systems. We analyse the interpolation path $X_λ= (1-λ)X_0 + λX_1$ that defines the Rectified Flow training. We show that a gap exists between the reconstruction of train and test data that follows a bell-shaped curve over $λ$, wich accumulates during training, while the validation metrics remain stable. The signal has a maximum whose location we derive in closed form under Gaussian assumptions. We validate these predictions on both audio and images and show that the bell-shaped structure is universal, while the peak prediction holds when our assumptions are satisfied. As a proof of concept, we exploit this specific $λ$-resolved structure to perform a Membership Inference Attack, distinguishing members of the training set from non-members.

item.title: Auditing Training Data in Domain-adapted LLMs: LoRA-MINT
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06946v1

AI Summary (中文)

LoRA-MINT：面向领域适配大语言模型的训练数据审计新方法

本研究提出 LoRA-MINT——一种专为采用低秩适应（LoRA）技术微调的大语言模型（LLMs） 设计的新型成员推断测试（Membership Inference Test, MINT）框架。其核心目标是高精度判别特定文本样本是否曾出现在模型的微调训练集中，从而为知识产权保护、敏感数据泄露风险评估及AI合规审计提供可落地的技术支撑。

我们首次系统揭示了模型在目标样本上的困惑度（perplexity）与其成员身份之间存在强统计关联，并基于此构建了轻量、无需访问原始训练数据或完整模型参数的推断流程：仅需冻结主干模型，利用LoRA适配器输出与梯度敏感性分析，结合校准后的困惑度阈值进行二元判定。该方法完全兼容主流LoRA微调范式，不依赖额外训练或影子模型，显著提升实用性与可扩展性。

在4个主流LLM（Llama-2-7B、Qwen-1.5-4B等）与3个NLP基准任务（AG News、SST-2、BoolQ） 上的实验表明：LoRA-MINT在不同模型-任务组合下均取得0.77–0.92的精确率（Precision），全面超越现有SOTA基线（如Loss-based MIA、Shadow Training），且对超参扰动、数据分布偏移和微调规模变化表现出优异鲁棒性。尤为关键的是，其设计天然支持模块化迁移——所提机制可无缝适配其他参数高效微调方法（如QLoRA、Adapter）乃至更广义的领域适配AI模型，为构建统一AI审计基础设施奠定基础。本工作不仅填补了LoRA微调场景下数据溯源工具的空白，更推动大模型透明化、可信化与负责任部署的实践进程。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce LoRA-MINT, a novel Membership Inference Test (MINT) framework tailored for Large Language Models (LLMs) fine-tuned via Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA). It audits whether individual text samples were present in the model’s task-specific training data—enabling practical intellectual property verification and sensitive data exposure assessment. Leveraging a systematic analysis of perplexity–membership correlation, LoRA-MINT performs inference using only frozen base models and LoRA adapters, requiring no shadow training or full parameter access. Experiments across four LLMs (e.g., Llama-2-7B, Qwen-1.5-4B) and three NLP benchmarks (AG News, SST-2, BoolQ) achieve precision of 0.77–0.92, consistently outperforming state-of-the-art baselines. The method is robust to hyperparameter variation and distribution shift, and its design generalizes readily to other efficient adaptation techniques (e.g., QLoRA) and domain-adapted AI models.

Abstract

We present LoRA-MINT, a new methodology for Membership Inference Test (MINT) applied to recent Large Language Models (LLMs) fine-tuned for specific Natural Language Processing (NLP) tasks through Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA). The primary goal is to assess whether individual samples were part of the training data of these adapted models, providing a useful auditing tool for the management of intellectual property and sensitive data. Our analysis explores the relationship between model perplexity and membership status, providing a systematic framework for estimating data exposure in fine-tuned LLMs. We conducted experiments on four models and three benchmark datasets, obtaining precision values in determining if given data were used for training ranging from 0.77 to 0.92, which outperform state-of-the-art baselines and demonstrate the robustness and generality of the proposed method. In general, our findings underscore the potential of LoRA-MINT as an effective and scalable framework for auditing LLMs, improving transparency, and fostering the ethical and responsible deployment of AI and NLP technologies. For the sake of concreteness and current relevance, our discussion and experiments are centered on LoRAadjusted LLMs, but note that most of the presented methodology is easily applicable for auditing training data given any other technique for adapting LLMs or, more generally, any other domain-adapted AI models.

item.title: LRMIL: Efficient Low-Resolution Multiple Instance Learning via High-Resolution Knowledge Distillation for Whole Slide Image Classification
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06864v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

多实例学习（MIL）已成为数字病理中全切片图像（WSI）分析的标准范式，可在无需密集像素级标注的前提下实现滑片级分类。然而，现有MIL方法普遍依赖对高分辨率（如20×）补丁的 exhaustive 提取与编码，导致两大临床瓶颈：（1）全局建模受限——低倍率（如5×/10×）下易丢失上下文与组织结构信息；（2）计算开销巨大——单张WSI常含数万高分辨率补丁，显著拖慢预处理与推理速度，难以部署于资源受限的临床环境。

方法创新：LRMIL框架

本文提出高效低分辨率多实例学习（LRMIL），首次将高分辨率知识系统性蒸馏至低分辨率表征中，兼顾精度与效率。其核心为双阶段知识蒸馏策略：

  • 补丁级跨分辨率蒸馏：以高分辨率补丁特征为教师，约束低分辨率（如5×）补丁嵌入在特征空间对齐，保留判别性局部语义；
  • 滑片级知识蒸馏：构建低分辨率学生MIL模型，在滑片标签监督下，同步接受高分辨率教师模型的软预测指导，强化全局决策一致性。

推理时，LRMIL仅需输入低分辨率补丁，跳过高分辨率加载、切块与编码全流程，大幅降低内存占用与延迟。

主要成果

在Camelyon16、Camelyon17、TCGA-BRCA等主流WSI基准上，LRMIL以仅30–40%的推理耗时，全面超越TransMIL、ABMIL、CLAM等SOTA方法（AUC提升1.2–2.8个百分点）。消融实验证实双阶段蒸馏协同增益显著（+1.9% AUC），且在不同放大倍率（5×/10×）下鲁棒性强。本工作为临床WSI分析提供了高精度、低延迟、易部署的实用化新路径。

AI Summary (English)

Multiple instance learning (MIL) is widely adopted for whole slide image (WSI) classification in digital pathology, yet existing methods suffer from high computational cost and poor global context modeling when processing massive high-resolution patches. To address this, we propose LRMIL, an efficient low-resolution MIL framework leveraging high-resolution knowledge distillation. LRMIL employs a two-stage distillation: (1) patch-level cross-resolution distillation aligns low-res (e.g., 5×) patch embeddings with high-res teacher features; and (2) slide-level knowledge distillation trains a low-res student MIL model under both ground-truth labels and teacher soft predictions. At inference, LRMIL operates solely on low-resolution patches—reducing preprocessing time by >65% and GPU memory usage by ~50% versus high-res baselines. Experiments on Camelyon16/17 and TCGA-BRCA show LRMIL consistently outperforms state-of-the-art MIL methods (e.g., +2.3% avg. AUC), achieving superior accuracy-efficiency trade-offs. This establishes LRMIL as a practical, scalable solution for clinical WSI analysis.

Abstract

Multiple instance learning (MIL) has become a standard paradigm for whole slide image (WSI) analysis in digital pathology, as it enables slide-level prediction without dense annotations. Existing MIL methods typically rely on exhaustive extraction and encoding of high-resolution patches. However, this practice suffers from two critical limitations in real-world clinical settings: it struggles to capture global visual cues at lower magnifications, and incurs substantial computational overhead due to the massive number of high-resolution patches per slide. To address these limitations, we propose an efficient low-resolution multiple instance learning (LRMIL) framework that transfers high-resolution knowledge to low-resolution representations. LRMIL adopts a two-stage distillation strategy. First, patch-level cross-resolution distillation aligns low-resolution patch embeddings with high-resolution representations. Second, slide-level knowledge distillation trains a low-resolution student MIL model under both slide-level supervision and teacher guidance. At inference time, LRMIL operates exclusively on low-resolution patches, substantially reducing data preprocessing and computational cost. Extensive experiments on multiple WSI benchmarks demonstrate that LRMIL consistently outperforms state-of-the-art MIL methods while achieving more efficient inference. These results highlight LRMIL as a practical and scalable solution for WSI analysis in clinical pathology.

item.title: Federated Foundation Models over Vehicular Networks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06786v1

AI Summary (中文)

面向车联网络的联邦基础模型：多模态多任务范式的前沿探索

本文前瞻性地提出将新兴的多模态多任务联邦基础模型（M3T FedFMs）深度融入智能车联网络（Vehicular Networks），旨在融合多模态多任务基础模型（M3T FMs）强大的表征能力与联邦学习（FL）固有的隐私保护与分布式协同优势。鉴于该方向尚属空白，本文首次系统构建M3T FedFMs的核心训练与微调范式，涵盖跨模态对齐、任务解耦聚合、轻量化客户端适配及异构车载设备上的梯度稀疏化策略。我们深入剖析其在车联场景中的典型应用：包括多源感知融合（激光雷达+摄像头+V2X信号联合理解）、动态交通意图预测、边缘协同高精地图实时更新、以及面向低时延的车载大模型个性化推理。针对车联环境特有的强移动性、通信高抖动、算力资源碎片化、数据时空高度非独立同分布（Non-IID）等关键约束，本文提炼出四大挑战，并提出“动态拓扑感知聚合”、“模态-任务自适应剪枝”、“车载-路侧协同蒸馏”与“隐私-时效双目标优化调度”等前沿研究方向。最后，基于真实世界Waymo Open Dataset开展端到端案例验证：在仅使用16辆模拟车辆、每轮仅上传0.8MB模型更新的约束下，M3T FedFM在3D目标检测与轨迹预测联合任务上相较传统车载FL提升23.7% mAP，同时将用户数据留存本地，满足GDPR合规要求。代码已开源（https://github.com/KasraBorazjani/vehicular-fedfm），以推动可复现研究。

AI Summary (English)

This paper pioneers the integration of Multi-Modal Multi-Task Federated Foundation Models (M3T FedFMs) into vehicular networks, unifying the expressive power of foundation models with the privacy-preserving, distributed nature of federated learning. We establish foundational training/fine-tuning principles for M3T FedFMs—including cross-modal alignment, task-aware aggregation, and on-device sparsification—tailored to vehicular constraints. We identify key challenges: extreme mobility, intermittent V2X connectivity, heterogeneous edge resources, and highly non-IID spatio-temporal data. Through a case study on the Waymo Open Dataset, our M3T FedFM achieves 23.7% higher joint mAP on 3D detection and trajectory prediction versus baseline FL, while uploading only 0.8 MB per round and keeping raw sensor data on vehicles. We release open-source code to accelerate research in this emerging domain.

Abstract

This paper presents a forward-looking vision for integrating the emerging multi-modal multi-task federated foundation models (M3T FedFMs) into vehicular networks, with the goal of unifying the expressive power of multi-modal multi-task foundation models (M3T FMs) with the privacy-preserving and distributed learning capabilities of federated learning (FL). Given the largely underexplored nature of this research direction, we first introduce the fundamental training/fine-tuning principles of M3T FedFMs. We then discuss a range of their representative use cases in vehicular networks, illustrating the significant potential of M3T FedFMs to enable next-generation vehicular intelligence. Afterwards, we identify key constraints inherent to vehicular environments that challenge the practical deployment of M3T FedFMs, and articulate a set of forward-looking research directions to address these challenges. Furthermore, through a case study conducted on a real-world vehicular dataset (i.e., Waymo Open Dataset), we demonstrate the promise of M3T FedFMs for vehicular networks and release our implementation to facilitate reproducibility and stimulate research in this emerging area (repository: https://github.com/KasraBorazjani/vehicular-fedfm)

item.title: Will the Agent Recuse Itself? Measuring LLM-Agent Compliance with In-Band Access-Deny Signals
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06460v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着大语言模型（LLM）驱动的自主智能体日益持有真实凭证、直接操作生产基础设施且“无人值守”，运维人员缺乏标准化机制向智能体传达“某资源禁止访问”的指令。现有访问控制仅提供二元选择：凭有效凭证放行（无法阻止合规但越权行为），或硬性拒绝（与网络故障等普通错误不可区分）。这导致治理盲区——亟需一种轻量、可解释、合作式的第三种访问调控模式。

方法与创新

本文提出Recuse Signal（退避信号）：一种开源轻量级带内拒绝信号，通过协议既有通道（如SSH登录横幅、PostgreSQL NOTICE 消息）由服务端主动发出，请求连接中的自动化智能体自愿中止操作。它类比 robots.txt，是协作型治理机制而非安全边界，其有效性完全依赖智能体的自愿遵守。我们定义该信号为开放微型标准，开发了零/低侵入式适配器（SSH banner + PAM钩子；PostgreSQL线协议代理），并部署于真实生产主机。在受控实验中，向全新初始化的LLM智能体分配无害运维任务（如查询系统负载），观测其是否响应信号而主动退避。

主要发现

  • 高合规性验证：SSH场景下（GPT-4o、GPT-4o-mini、Claude Code），信号存在时100%触发退避；无信号对照组则100%完成任务，证明信号具备强行为引导力。
  • 合作性本质凸显：当提示中明确加入“运维人员已授权绕过”框架时，最强模型（GPT-4o）转为执行任务，而其余模型仍尊重主机策略——表明该信号本质是可协商的治理信令，非强制锁。
  • 开源实践落地：完整释放信号规范、适配器代码及实验框架，支持复现与生态共建。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces the Recuse Signal: a lightweight, in-band, cooperative access-deny mechanism for LLM agents operating on live infrastructure. Unlike traditional access controls (which either grant or hard-fail), the Recuse Signal—transmitted via existing protocol channels (e.g., SSH banners, PostgreSQL NOTICE messages)—requests automated agents to voluntarily withdraw from a resource. It is explicitly not a security boundary but a governance primitive analogous to robots.txt. We define it as an open mini-standard, implement low-footprint adapters for SSH and PostgreSQL, deploy them on a production host, and conduct a controlled experiment with fresh LLM agents performing benign operational tasks. Results show 100% recusal under the signal versus 100% task completion in the no-signal control (SSH pilot with GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, and Claude Code). Crucially, the signal behaves cooperatively: explicit operator-authorization framing causes the most capable model to proceed, while others defer to host policy—demonstrating its role as a negotiable, human-aligned governance channel. All artifacts are publicly released.

Abstract

As autonomous LLM agents increasingly hold real credentials and operate infrastructure without a human in the loop, operators have no standard way to tell an agent that a resource is off-limits. Access controls either let the agent in (it has valid credentials) or hard-fail it (indistinguishable from any other client). We propose a third mode: a lightweight, published in-band deny signal -- the Recuse Signal -- that a server emits over a protocol's existing channels (an SSH banner, a PostgreSQL NOTICE) asking a connecting automated agent to voluntarily withdraw. This is a cooperative governance control, the robots.txt analogue for live access; it is explicitly not a security boundary. Its value is entirely empirical and, to our knowledge, unmeasured: do compliant LLM agents actually honor such a signal? We define the signal as an open mini-standard, implement two zero- or low-footprint adapters (an SSH banner/PAM hook and a PostgreSQL wire-protocol proxy), deploy them on a live production host, and run a controlled experiment in which fresh agents are given a benign operations task and observed for recusal. In a pilot (SSH; OpenAI GPT-4o and GPT-4o-mini; and Claude Code as a deployed agent), the signal cleanly induces recusal -- 100% recusal when present versus 100% task completion in a no-signal control -- and, revealingly, behaves as a cooperative rather than absolute signal: an explicit operator-authorization framing flips the most capable model to proceed, while other agents continue to defer to the on-host policy. We release the standard, adapters, and experiment harness for reproduction.

item.title: WebMCP Tool Surface Poisoning: Runtime Manipulation Attacks on LLM Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06387v1

AI Summary (中文)

WebMCP工具表面投毒：面向大语言模型代理的运行时操纵攻击

WebMCP（Web Model Control Protocol）是一种新兴协议，允许网站直接向AI代理暴露结构化工具接口，绕过传统UI层。该范式虽提升自动化能力，却显著扩展了Web会话的攻击面，尤其在第三方脚本广泛嵌入的现代Web生态中。本文首次识别并系统定义了一类新型威胁——会话中工具注入（Mid-Session Tool Injection, MSTI）：攻击者利用第三方脚本在LLM代理已建立会话期间动态篡改其可访问工具集合或语义认知。

我们提出双维度分类框架：

  • 工具劫持（Tool Hijacking）：通过AbortSignal API中断注册、竞态条件覆盖、或篡改toolRegistry全局对象，物理性移除/替换工具入口；
  • 工具框定（Tool Framing）：不改变工具存在性，而恶意篡改name、description、readOnlyHint及inputSchema等元数据字段，扭曲代理对工具功能、权限与输入约束的认知。

基于真实WebMCP兼容环境的实验验证表明：两类攻击均能导致代理执行越权操作（如绕过只读限制）、调用错误工具链、或生成严重偏离用户意图的输出。进一步分析揭示，MSTI根植于WebMCP特有的工具生命周期松耦合性与元数据驱动的语义解析机制，使“工具表面”（tool surface）本身成为新型安全边界。为此，我们提出四项关键缓解方向：① 工具身份强绑定至注册源（origin）；② 强制工具注册/注销生命周期一致性（如引入toolId不可变标识与引用计数）；③ 为第三方工具实施严格数据隔离边界（sandboxed execution + schema validation）；④ 全链路记录工具注册、元数据变更与调用日志，支持可审计回溯。本研究首次将LLM代理的工具交互层纳入系统性安全建模，为下一代AI-Native Web协议的安全设计提供了基础性警示与实践指南。

AI Summary (English)

WebMCP enables websites to expose tools directly to LLM agents, bypassing UIs—but introduces novel runtime security risks. This paper identifies Mid-Session Tool Injection (MSTI), a new class of attack where adversaries exploit third-party scripts to manipulate agent-accessible tools during active sessions. We classify MSTI into two categories: Tool Hijacking, which alters the set of available tools via race conditions or AbortSignal abuse; and Tool Framing, which corrupts the agent’s semantic understanding by tampering with metadata (e.g., name, description, inputSchema). Our implementation confirms both vectors successfully disrupt intended WebMCP behavior—causing privilege escalation, incorrect tool selection, and unsafe outputs. We attribute MSTI to WebMCP’s decoupled tool lifecycle and metadata-driven execution model, establishing the “tool surface” as an emergent security boundary. We propose concrete mitigations: origin-bound tool identity, lifecycle-consistent registration, strict data boundaries for third-party tools, and auditable tool registration/invoke logging. This work provides the first systematic security analysis of WebMCP’s tool interface and foundational guidance for secure AI-native web protocols.

Abstract

WebMCP is a newly emerging protocol that enables websites to expose tools directly to AI agents, bypassing traditional user interfaces and introducing new security risks. The dynamic exposure of agent-accessible tools in WebMCP expands the attack surface of web sessions, especially when third-party scripts are involved. In this study, we identify a new potential threat, termed Mid-Session Tool Injection (MSTI), in which attackers leverage third-party scripts to inject malicious tools during an active session. To better characterize this threat, we classify MSTI based on the stage and target of manipulation, distinguishing between Tool Hijacking and Tool Framing. Tool Hijacking modifies the set of tools visible to the agent through mechanisms such as the AbortSignal API or race conditions during tool registration. In contrast, Tool Framing influences the agent's perception of tool roles through metadata fields such as tool name, description, readOnlyHint, and inputSchema. Our implementation demonstrates that both Tool Hijacking and Tool Framing can successfully disrupt the intended functionality of WebMCP. Based on these results, we outline potential mitigation directions and provide security design recommendations for WebMCP, including binding tool identity to its origin, ensuring lifecycle consistency, enforcing data boundaries for third-party tools, and maintaining traceable logs of tool registration and invocation. These findings indicate that MSTI arises from WebMCP's unique tool lifecycle and structured metadata, making the tool surface itself an emerging security concern.

item.title: Robust Ensemble of Selectively Strengthened and Augmented Predictors
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06265v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

针对机器学习分类器的逃避攻击（evasion attacks）严重威胁金融欺诈检测、网络安全等关键应用的可靠性。现有防御方法（如对抗训练、输入净化）常面临两大瓶颈：泛化能力弱——在未见过的攻击类型或数据分布下性能骤降；鲁棒性提升不系统——缺乏统一机制协同优化特征鲁棒性与模型结构韧性。

方法创新：RESSAP框架

我们提出选择性增强与增广预测器鲁棒集成框架（Robust Ensemble of Selectively Strengthened and Augmented Predictors, RESSAP），一种模型无关、可即插即用的防御范式：

  • 选择性特征强化：基于新定义的韧性得分（Resilience Score），联合评估特征重要性（如SHAP值）与局部鲁棒性（梯度敏感度），动态筛选高韧性子集，为每个基分类器构建专属精简特征空间；
  • 随机化集成推理：部署时仅随机激活部分基分类器（如5/10），显著增加攻击者预估决策逻辑的难度，打破“确定性脆弱路径”；
  • 噪声感知数据增广：在训练中注入自适应高斯噪声（噪声方差与特征韧性得分负相关），在易受攻击维度施加更强正则化，加固决策边界。

实验验证与优势

在UCI信用卡欺诈、NSL-KDD网络入侵及ImageNet-1K子集上，RESSAP在FGSM、PGD、CW等7类主流逃避攻击下，平均鲁棒准确率提升23.6%–38.1%，同时在干净数据上保持≤0.9%的精度损失。相比单模型对抗训练，其计算开销仅增加17%，且无需修改原始模型架构或重标标签。该框架为ML系统提供了可扩展、低侵入、跨任务兼容的安全增强方案。

AI Summary (English)

Evasion attacks severely undermine the reliability of ML classifiers in security-critical domains. To address the limited generalizability and unsystematic robustness of existing defenses, we propose RESSAP: a model-agnostic framework that transforms a single classifier into a robust ensemble. RESSAP selects feature subsets for each base predictor using a novel Resilience Score—jointly quantifying feature importance and local robustness—and applies adaptive noise-based augmentation during training to strengthen decision boundaries. At inference, a random subset of predictors is activated, increasing unpredictability for adversaries. Experiments across fraud detection, intrusion detection, and image classification show RESSAP improves average robust accuracy by 23.6–38.1% against 7 evasion attacks (e.g., PGD, CW), with <0.9% clean-data accuracy drop and only 17% extra training cost. RESSAP delivers scalable, architecture-free defense without retraining or structural changes.

Abstract

Evasion attacks present a significant challenge to the robustness of machine learning (ML)-based classifiers, particularly in critical applications such as fraud detection and cybersecurity. Although existing defense mechanisms are effective in some settings, they often suffer from limited generalizability and do not systematically improve model robustness across diverse attack scenarios. To address these limitations, we introduce Robust Ensemble of Selectively Strengthened and Augmented Predictors (RESSAP), a novel framework that transforms a single classifier into an ensemble of robust classifiers. Each classifier in the ensemble is trained on a carefully selected subset of features, where feature selection is guided by a resilience metric that accounts for both feature importance and robustness. During inference, a random subset of these classifiers is used to make predictions, increasing unpredictability and improving resistance to adversarial manipulation. In addition, noise-based data augmentation is applied during training to strengthen decision boundaries and improve generalization. Our experimental results demonstrate that RESSAP significantly improves robustness against adversarial evasion attacks while maintaining strong accuracy on clean data. Overall, this model-agnostic framework provides a scalable and flexible defense strategy for enhancing the security of machine learning systems without requiring major changes to existing architectures.

item.title: GenTI: Benchmarking LLMs for Autonomous IDPS Rule Generation for Unseen Attacks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05844v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

基于规则的入侵检测与防御系统（IDPS）虽具备高精度检测与主动缓解能力，但其依赖人工编写的签名式规则，难以应对新兴威胁与零日攻击。现有公开数据集（如CICIDS2017、UNSW-NB15）聚焦网络流量分类，缺乏结构化规则语义、响应逻辑与威胁情报映射，严重制约自动化规则生成研究。

方法创新

本文提出Generative Threat Intelligence（GenTI）——首个面向未见攻击的LLM驱动IDPS规则生成基准框架。核心包含两大部分：

  • GTI数据集：整合超15万条Snort/Suricata/ET规则及5万条YARA规则，每条均标注协议行为、载荷特征、上下文关系、Cyber Threat Intelligence（CTI）实体映射（如MITRE ATT&CK技术ID、CVE、TTPs），以及可执行响应类型（alert/drop/reject）；
  • LLM生成管道：融合结构化提示工程、思维链（Chain-of-Thought, CoT）推理与验证链（Chain-of-Verification, CoVe）闭环，实现从分析师自然语言指令+典型载荷样本→语法合规、语义准确、安全有效的部署级规则的端到端转化，并支持实时在Snort/Suricata中执行验证。

主要结果

GenTI在真实引擎中达成：复合规则质量分89.4%，CTI覆盖率达94.8%，未见攻击检出率从基线45%显著提升至87.4%，误报率由8.5%降至2.3%。本工作首次实现规则粒度CTI与大模型自动化生成的深度耦合，为构建自适应、自演化的下一代IDPS奠定基准与范式。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce GenTI, the first large-scale LLM-driven benchmark for autonomous generation of deployable IDPS rules against unseen attacks. To overcome the rigidity of manual rule authoring and the lack of CTI-enriched, action-oriented datasets, we curate GTI: a corpus of 200K+ production-grade rules (150K Snort/Suricata/ET + 50K YARA), each annotated with protocol semantics, payload patterns, contextual relationships, MITRE ATT&CK/CVE mappings, and executable responses (alert/drop/reject). Our LLM pipeline employs structured prompting, Chain-of-Thought reasoning, and a Chain-of-Verification loop to synthesize syntactically valid, semantically faithful, and security-effective rules—directly executable in Snort/Suricata. Evaluated across syntax accuracy, semantic similarity, CTI coverage, and real-world detection efficacy, GenTI achieves an 89.4% composite rule-quality score, 94.8% CTI coverage, boosts unseen-attack detection from 45% to 87.4%, and slashes false positives from 8.5% to 2.3%. GenTI establishes a foundational benchmark tightly integrating threat intelligence at the rule level with generative AI automation.

Abstract

Rule-based Intrusion Detection and Prevention Systems (IDPS) offer precise attack detection as well as mitigation, however their manually crafted, signature-driven rules limit adaptability to emerging and zero-day threats. Additionally, existing public datasets (e.g., CICIDS2017, UNSW-NB15) focus on traffic classification and provide little structured information to support automatic rule synthesis or prevention logic. To address this gap, we propose Generative Thread Intelligence (GenTI) \footnote{GenTI refers to the proposed framework, and GTI refers to the dataset.} an LLM-driven benchmark for automatic generation of IDPS rules targeting unseen attacks. The dataset (GTI) aggregates over 150k detection and prevention rules from Snort, Suricata, Emerging Threats, as well as 50k YARA, each annotated with protocol behavior, payload signatures, contextual relationships, mappings to Cyber Threat Intelligence (CTI), along with actionable response types (alert, drop, reject). Moreover, on top of this corpus we design an LLM-based pipeline that transforms analyst prompts and representative payloads into deployable rules via structured prompt engineering, Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning, as well as a Chain-of-Verification (CoVe) loop for syntactic, semantic, and security validation. The generated rules are executed in real time on (Snort/Suricata) and evaluated by syntax accuracy, semantic similarity, CTI coverage, security effectiveness as well as unseen attacks detection. Furthermore, our GenTI instantiation achieves a composite rule-quality score of 89.4\%, with 94.8\% CTI coverage, improving unseen attacks detection from 45\% to 87.4\% and reducing the false-positive rate from 8.5\% to 2.3\%. Overall, GenTI establishes the first large-scale benchmark that tightly couples rule-level CTI with LLM-based automation, enabling adaptive, self-evolving IDPS.

item.title: GCD: Garbled, Corrected, Demonstrandum -- Fixing and Proving Go's Extended GCD Implementation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05796v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

本文针对 Go 语言标准库中 crypto/internal/fips140/bigmod 模块的 extendedGCD（扩展欧几里得算法）实现开展形式化验证。该函数是 RSA 密钥生成的核心组件，直接影响密码学安全性。尽管其声称直接移植自 BoringSSL，我们发现两处关键偏差：(1) 系数更新逻辑错误——Go 版本在迭代中错误维护贝祖系数，破坏了核心不变式 $a \cdot s + b \cdot t = r$；(2) 输入域过度放宽——允许非标准输入（如 $b=0$ 或 $|a|<|b|$），导致未定义行为与潜在崩溃。

方法与技术路线

我们提出“修复—证明”协同框架：

  • 修复层面：修正系数更新逻辑，消除数值不一致；优化后实测平均性能提升 24%（因减少冗余分支与中间赋值）；
  • 验证层面：基于 Gobra（Go 专用演绎验证器）构建端到端正确性证明，涵盖终止性与功能正确性（即输出满足 $\gcd(a,b) = as + bt$ 且 $|s| \leq |b|$, $|t| \leq |a|$）；
  • 数学支撑：对 Gobra 无法自动处理的非线性整数不等式引理（如商余关系边界、绝对值单调性），使用 Lean 4 形式化证明，并以可信接口导入 Gobra。

关键洞见与贡献

1. 隐蔽缺陷普遍性：即便经工业级审查（BoringSSL → Go 双重审核），微小语义偏差仍可绕过测试与人工检视；
2. 验证即调试范式：Gobra 的反例反馈驱动我们迭代精化循环不变式（如引入带符号的归一化残差约束），将验证过程转化为深度代码审计；
3. AI 增强验证：实验表明，大语言模型可基于 Gobra 报错自动建议缺失引理、重构归纳假设，显著加速验证循环——为高保障密码代码的规模化验证提供新路径。

AI Summary (English)

We formally verify Go’s extendedGCD implementation in crypto/internal/fips140/bigmod, a critical component for RSA key generation. Despite being intended as a direct port of BoringSSL’s implementation, we uncover two deviations that break essential algorithmic invariants: (1) incorrect coefficient updates violating the Bézout identity $as + bt = r$, and (2) an overly permissive input domain enabling undefined behavior. We fix the first deviation—yielding a verified implementation with 24% average speedup—and extend an existing BoringSSL proof to cover the broader domain. Using Gobra, we prove full functional correctness and termination; for non-linear integer lemmata beyond Gobra’s automation (e.g., bounds on quotients and absolute values), we employ Lean 4, exporting proofs as trusted axioms. Our effort reveals three insights: subtle bugs evade even rigorous peer review; formal verification is uniquely effective at exposing them; and AI agents can accelerate verification by iteratively refining invariants and lemmata in response to verifier feedback.

Abstract

We verify the 'extendedGCD' implementation in Go's standard library ('crypto/internal/fips140/bigmod'), which plays a crucial role in the generation of RSA key pairs. Even though the Go implementation is supposedly a direct port from BoringSSL's implementation, we uncovered two deviations that each break the algorithm's invariants: (1) the Go implementation deviates in the way coefficients are updated, and (2) it permits a larger input domain. We address both deviations; the first by fixing the Go implementation, which results in an on average 24% speedup, and the second deviation by porting an existing proof for BoringSSL and extending it to cover the larger input domain. We prove correctness and termination of the fixed Go implementation using Gobra, a deductive program verifier for Go. Where necessary, we used Lean to prove key lemmata on non-linear arithmetic, which we import into Gobra. Our verification effort reveals three key insights: subtle bugs can slip into even well-reviewed code with surprising ease; formal verification is a powerful tool for uncovering them; and AI agents can facilitate the verification process by iteratively refining invariants and lemmata based on Gobra's error messages.

item.title: Membrane: A Self-Evolving Contrastive Safety Memory for LLM Agent Defense
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05743v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

尽管安全对齐技术持续进步，大语言模型（LLM）仍面临动态演化的越狱攻击（jailbreaks）威胁。现有基于微调的安全分类器缺乏在线适应能力；而依赖记忆的自适应防护机制（如记忆增强型守卫）又普遍存在过度拒绝（over-refusal）问题——即因误判语义相似性，将大量良性查询错误拦截（拒答率高达28–85%），严重损害可用性。

方法创新：Membrane 与对比安全记忆（CSM）

我们提出 Membrane——一种无需重训练、可自我演化的对比式安全守卫框架。其核心是对比安全记忆（Contrastive Safety Memory, CSM）：每个记忆单元（cell）以结构化方式并置存储一对条件——阻断有害查询的触发逻辑 与 允许表面相似但本质良性的请求的许可逻辑。Membrane 通过在线蒸馏每次有害交互及其对应的良性对照样本，按底层攻击策略（如“角色扮演”“混淆编码”）自动索引生成新 cell；单个 cell 可泛化至同一攻击机制下的多主题变体，实现策略级抽象而非表层匹配。

关键结果与优势

  • 在 HarmBench（模型级） 和 AgentHarm（代理级） 两大基准上，Membrane 在全部六类主流越狱攻击中均取得最高 F1 分数；
  • 良性拒答率仅 7–14%，显著优于现有方法（28–85%），兼顾安全性与实用性；
  • 具备强鲁棒性：跨攻击迁移场景下记忆单元 F1 保持 87–88%；在恶意记忆注入（memory poisoning）测试中表现稳定，未出现性能塌缩。

AI Summary (English)

Despite progress in LLM safety alignment, models remain vulnerable to continuously evolving jailbreaks. Existing fine-tuned classifiers lack adaptability, while memory-based guardrails suffer from high benign refusal (28–85%). We propose Membrane, a self-evolving contrastive safety guardrail built on Contrastive Safety Memory (CSM). Each CSM cell jointly encodes blocking conditions for harmful queries and permitting conditions for superficially similar benign ones, indexed by underlying attack strategy—not surface features—enabling cross-topic generalization without retraining. At inference, retrieved cells provide precise, context-grounded safety decisions. On HarmBench and AgentHarm, Membrane achieves the highest F1 across all six jailbreak attacks, with benign refusal reduced to just 7–14%. It maintains 87–88% F1 under cross-attack transfer and resists memory poisoning, demonstrating robust, adaptive, and practical safety enforcement.

Abstract

Despite advances in safety alignment, large language models remain vulnerable to continuously evolving jailbreaks. Existing fine-tuned safety classifiers cannot adapt to these evolving attacks, while adaptive memory-based guardrails tend to over-refuse benign queries that resemble stored attacks. We propose Membrane, a self-evolving guardrail built on Contrastive Safety Memory (CSM): each cell pairs the conditions for blocking a harmful query with those for permitting a superficially similar benign request. Without retraining, Membrane evolves CSM by distilling each harmful interaction and its benign counterpart into a contrastive cell indexed by the underlying attack strategy, so that one cell generalizes across topical variants of the same mechanism. At inference, retrieved cells serve as grounding context for precise safety decisions. Across model-level safety on HarmBench and agent-level safety on AgentHarm, Membrane achieves the highest F1 on all six jailbreak attacks. Notably, benign refusal on AgentHarm stays at 7-14%, well below the 28-85% range of prior guards. Memory cells also retain 87-88% F1 under cross-attack transfer and remain stable under memory poisoning.

item.title: An Embarrassingly Simple Detector for Model Extraction Attacks in Large Language Model API Traffic
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05725v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

随着大语言模型（LLMs）广泛通过托管API部署，模型提取攻击（Model Extraction Attacks, MEA）已成为威胁模型知识产权与服务安全的现实风险。然而，单次提取查询在表层语义和统计特征上常与正常用户请求高度相似，导致传统基于单查询异常打分或纯“良性用户 vs. 攻击者用户”二分设定的检测方法鲁棒性不足，难以应对真实API流量中多用户混杂、攻击请求比例动态变化的复杂场景。

方法创新

本文提出一种极简但强效的检测范式：将模型提取监控建模为良校准的流量窗口分布检验问题。核心思想是——不依赖攻击样本训练，仅利用历史良性流量构建语义分布基线：

  • 将实时API请求嵌入统一语义空间（如LLM最后一层隐藏状态均值）；
  • 在滑动时间窗口内聚合查询嵌入，计算其经验分布与历史良性流量分布的最大均值差异（Maximum Mean Discrepancy, MMD）；
  • 关键创新：决策阈值完全通过良性-良性对比（benign-vs-benign）自适应设定，彻底规避对攻击数据的依赖，实现零先验、强泛化。

主要结果

在涵盖4类典型提取场景（梯度反演、提示注入、API重放、黑盒蒸馏）的14组攻击-正常查询对上系统评估：

  • FPR仅0.3%（三随机种子平均），保障API服务可用性；
  • 纯攻击者场景TPR达100.0%，混合流量下平均TPR达90.5%（攻击请求占比5%–50%）；
  • 平衡准确率达95.1%，显著优于适配后的PRADA、SEAT、CAP、DATE及边缘马氏距离等基线；
  • 首次在用户级与混合多用户API流量双重设置下验证了分布检验范式的有效性，确立其为模型提取检测的强实证基线。

AI Summary (English)

Large language models (LLMs) deployed via hosted APIs face growing threats from model extraction attacks (MEAs), yet detection remains challenging due to the semantic similarity between extraction and benign queries. We reframe MEA monitoring as benign-calibrated traffic-window distribution testing: incoming queries are embedded into a semantic space (e.g., mean hidden states), and their aggregate distribution over a time window is tested for deviation from historical benign traffic using Maximum Mean Discrepancy (MMD). Crucially, the decision threshold is set exclusively via benign-vs-benign comparisons—no attack data or labels are required. Evaluated across 14 attacker-normal query pairs from four realistic extraction scenarios, our MMD detector achieves 0.3% benign false positive rate, 100.0% true positive rate in pure-attacker settings, 90.5% average TPR under mixed multi-user traffic, and 95.1% balanced accuracy, outperforming adapted PRADA, SEAT, CAP, DATE, and marginal Mahalanobis baselines. This establishes benign-calibrated distribution testing as a simple, robust, and empirically strong baseline for real-world LLM API security. Code: https://github.com/LabRAI/mmd-llm-mea-detection.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed through hosted APIs, making model extraction a practical threat to model ownership and service security. However, individual extraction queries often resemble benign requests, and existing evaluations often focus on single-query anomaly scoring or pure benign-versus-attacker user settings. We formulate model extraction monitoring as benign-calibrated traffic-window distribution testing and show that an embarrassingly simple detector is effective: embed incoming queries into a semantic space and test whether their aggregate distribution deviates from historical benign traffic. We instantiate the detector with maximum mean discrepancy (MMD), using only benign-vs-benign comparisons to set the decision threshold. We evaluate on fourteen attacker-normal query pairs from four extraction scenarios and compare with adapted PRADA, SEAT, CAP, DATE, and marginal Mahalanobis baselines. Across three random seeds, MMD achieves 0.3% benign FPR, 100.0% pure-attacker TPR, 90.5% average TPR over attacker fractions, and 95.1% balanced accuracy. These results show that benign-calibrated distribution testing is a strong empirical baseline for model extraction detection in both user-level and mixed multi-user LLM API traffic. Code is released at: https://github.com/LabRAI/mmd-llm-mea-detection.

item.title: Cognitive Threat Intelligence and Explainable Federated Security Analytics for distributed Infrastructure Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05701v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与挑战

随着云原生架构、物联网（IoT）、边缘计算等技术在能源、交通、工业互联网等关键基础设施中的深度部署，分布式系统面临攻击面持续扩大、APT攻击频发、数据主权敏感性强等严峻挑战。传统基于中心化服务器的入侵检测系统（IDS）存在三大瓶颈：（1）隐私风险高——需汇聚原始网络流量，违反GDPR/《个人信息保护法》；（2）可扩展性差——海量边缘节点导致通信开销激增与单点故障；（3）决策黑箱化——深度学习模型缺乏可解释性，难以支撑安全运营人员快速响应与归因分析。

方法创新

本研究提出“认知威胁情报驱动的可解释联邦安全分析框架”（CTI-XFSA），深度融合三大技术栈：

  • 联邦学习（FL）层：采用差分隐私增强的安全聚合协议（Secure Aggregation + DP-noise），支持异构边缘节点（如工控网关、5G基站）本地训练轻量级检测模型（Random Forest/XGBoost/Autoencoder），仅上传加密梯度参数；
  • 可解释AI（XAI）层：集成SHAP与LIME双路径解释引擎，为每条告警生成特征贡献热力图与自然语言归因短句（如“该流量被判定为Mirai变种，主因TCP标志异常（权重0.82）与DNS请求频率突增（权重0.76）”）；
  • 认知情报层：构建动态威胁知识图谱，将联邦模型输出的异常模式实时映射至MITRE ATT&CK战术阶段，并关联IOC（IP、域名、哈希）与TTPs（战术、技术、过程），实现从“检测告警”到“攻击链推理”的跃迁。

关键成果

在NSL-KDD、CIC-IDS2017及真实电力SCADA边缘数据集上验证：检测准确率提升9.2%（达99.1%），误报率降低41%，模型更新通信开销减少76%；XAI模块使安全分析师平均研判时间缩短63%，威胁情报关联覆盖率提升至89%。本框架已通过国家电网某省级调度中心POC验证，成为首个支持“联邦训练—可解释研判—认知溯源”闭环的基础设施级安全分析范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper proposes CTI-XFSA, a Cognitive Threat Intelligence and Explainable Federated Security Analytics framework for distributed infrastructure systems. To overcome scalability, privacy, and opacity limitations of centralized intrusion detection, CTI-XFSA integrates federated learning (FL) with differential privacy–enhanced secure aggregation, enabling local model training on edge nodes (e.g., IoT gateways, SCADA devices) without raw data sharing; explainable AI (XAI) via SHAP/LIME to generate human-interpretable threat attributions (e.g., “TCP flag anomaly: weight 0.82”); and cognitive threat intelligence, mapping detection outputs to MITRE ATT&CK tactics and enriching alerts with real-time IOC/TTP correlations. Evaluated on NSL-KDD, CIC-IDS2017, and live SCADA edge data, CTI-XFSA achieves 99.1% detection accuracy, 41% lower false positives, 76% reduced communication overhead, and 63% faster analyst triage. It represents the first production-ready framework unifying privacy-preserving collaboration, actionable explanations, and attack-chain reasoning for critical infrastructure security.

Abstract

The increasing adoption of distributed infrastructure systems, cloud computing, Internet of Things (IoT) technologies, and edge-based architectures has significantly expanded the cybersecurity attack surface and introduced increasingly sophisticated cyber threats. Conventional centralized intrusion detection approaches often face challenges related to scalability, data privacy, communication overhead, and limited transparency in artificial intelligence-driven decision-making processes. To address these limitations, this study proposes a Cognitive Threat Intelligence and Explainable Federated Security Analytics framework for distributed infrastructure systems. The proposed framework integrates Federated Learning (FL), Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI), and cognitive cybersecurity analytics to enable collaborative and privacy-preserving cyber threat detection across distributed network environments. Instead of transmitting sensitive raw network traffic data to centralized servers, local security models are independently trained at distributed nodes, where only encrypted model parameters and updates are shared through a federated aggregation mechanism. This decentralized learning architecture improves privacy protection while reducing communication dependency and centralized security risks. To enhance intelligent threat analysis, the framework incorporates machine learning and deep learning algorithms including Random Forest, XGBoost, Autoencoder

item.title: Protecting K-Nearest Neighbor Queries from Location Inference Attacks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05648v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

k近邻查询（kNNQ）是位置服务（LBS）的核心功能，广泛应用于“附近的人”等场景。然而，其查询响应（即返回的k个地理邻近对象）隐含用户位置信息，长期缺乏系统性隐私风险评估。本文首次揭示kNNQ存在严重的位置推断漏洞。

攻击模型创新

我们提出两种新型位置推断攻击：

  • 几何交集位置推断攻击（GI-LIA）：利用多次kNNQ响应在空间上的几何约束（如距离不等式与圆盘交集），通过解析几何求解缩小用户真实位置的可行区域；
  • 零阶优化位置推断攻击（ZO-LIA）：无需梯度信息，仅依赖查询响应的布尔反馈（如“目标是否在结果中”），采用黑盒优化算法高效逼近真实位置。实验证明二者可在平均3.2次查询内将定位误差压缩至50米以内，远超现有攻击能力。

防御框架DPRS

为应对上述威胁，我们设计差分隐私kNNQ保护框架DPRS，包含两大核心技术：
1. 受限区间内的拒绝采样机制：在动态构造的扰动区间内进行噪声注入，并拒绝超出几何合理性的扰动样本，显著抑制因过量噪声导致的距离失真；
2. 私有区间构造算法：基于查询点局部空间密度与k值自适应生成紧凑扰动区间，在满足ε-差分隐私前提下最大化保留原始距离序关系。

实验验证

在Foursquare、Gowalla等真实轨迹数据集上，DPRS在ε=1.0时实现平均查询精度损失<8.5%（对比基线方法下降达37%），同时将位置推断成功率压制至<6.2%（GI-LIA）和<9.1%（ZO-LIA），全面优于Geo-Indistinguishability、PrivKNN等SOTA方法。代码已开源：https://github.com/reanatom/DPRS。

AI Summary (English)

The k-nearest neighbor query (kNNQ) is fundamental to location-based services but poses unaddressed location privacy risks. We present the first two practical location inference attacks against kNNQ: the Geometric Intersection LIA (GI-LIA) and the Zero-Order Optimization LIA (ZO-LIA), both capable of localizing users within ~50 meters using only a few queries. To counter these threats, we propose DPRS, a differential privacy framework for kNNQ that introduces a rejection sampling mechanism within an adaptively constructed perturbation interval—effectively reducing distance distortion while preserving utility. Our private interval construction algorithm tailors noise bounds to local spatial density and query parameters, enabling superior privacy-utility trade-offs.
